one_line_description: Community chat platform with servers, channels, voice, and a massive bot ecosystem.
auth_summary: The API accepts an Authorization header with either a bot token ('Bot <token>' from the app's Bot page) or an OAuth2 bearer token ('Bearer <token>') for user-delegated access.
access_notes: Register an application self-serve in the Discord Developer Portal; bot token is issued immediately. Privileged gateway intents (message content, presence, members) and bots in 100+ servers require verification/approval.
api_surface_summary: HTTP REST API (discord.com/api) covering messages, channels, guilds, members, roles, threads, slash commands, plus a Gateway WebSocket API for real-time events.
agent_notes: Fetched official API reference (now hosted at docs.discord.com). Many community MCP servers exist; no official Discord MCP found. Caveat for a toolkit: message-content privileged intent and bot verification at scale, and the bot must be invited to each guild.
